### Post by IsKall on 2008-06-12
what I mean is, when I turn off the screen and wait for a couple of hours I cant see anything, my screen lamp is just "blinks" it should be on if there is a connection.

My screen: Samsung syncmaster BW226

Anyone why it does lose its connection to ubuntu?

my xorg.conf


### Post by HalPomeranz on 2008-06-12
I assume you've tried moving the mouse or hitting a key on the keyboard?  The power saving features in Linux will disable the monitor after some period of idle time, but should reactivate the monitor if you type something or move the mouse.

---

### Post by IsKall on 2008-06-12
Of course I did those, but still it is no connection. How can I fix this?

---

### Post by HalPomeranz on 2008-06-12
Some keyboard sequences I'd try:

1) Ctrl-Alt-F1 should take you to the text console, Ctrl-Alt-F7 will take you back to your X session.  Maybe this will wake up your monitor.

2) Ctrl-Alt-Backspace will terminate your current X session and relaunch the login screen.  Brutal, but it may cause your monitor to wake up.

Is the machine networked at all?  Can install an SSH server so that you can log in remotely and debug the problem when the monitor's hosed up?

---

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-06-23
Does it happen when the monitor goes on standby or just when you turn it off.

Try putting your standby timeout *(power management, display slider)* up all the way to never. Then turn your monitor off and see if it persists. Maybe it is happening because the card/monitor is going into standby while the power is off and making the monitor unable to detect the output when its switched back on.


Weird :confused:

---

### Post by IsKall on 2008-06-23
> **Tomatz said:**
> Does it happen when the monitor goes on standby or just when you turn it off.

Try putting your standby timeout *(power management, display slider)* up all the way to never. Then turn your monitor off and see if it persists. Maybe it is happening because the card/monitor is going into standby while the power is off and making the monitor unable to detect the output when its switched back on.


Weird :confused:

Thats whats happening! I have them already on never, but I think it has to do what you say, that monitor can not find the output. 

It only happens when the monitor is turned off for a while. The monitor tries to find it in digital/analog mode but then just turn it self to sleep mode again because it cant find the output. 

But why it does is really odd, is there drivers for this monitor in linux? Maybe it can be the monitor, but I think it has to do with hardy heron.

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-06-23
It is odd. I can't see anything wrong with your xorg.conf.


You could try using [envyng]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=571188") to reinstall your drivers it might fix the issue **_BUT_** _you run the risk of causing yourself more serious issues._


Does it produce the same results if the monitor just goes into standby (timeout)? if not just set it to go into standby as newer LCD monitors on standby consume very little power.

---

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-06-23
I've had a google but i can't find anything like it.

My monitor does this if i boot the pc with the monitor switched off but all i have to do is restart x.

Try this.

In a terminal type:
> 
gksu displayconfig-gtk

Then set up your monitor manually.

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-06-23
I found this:

> 
**HERE==>** [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Gutsy_Installation_Guide](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Gutsy_Installation_Guide)

Monitor detection (1680x1050 Resolution Issue)--- SOLVED 

As specified in the release notes, "Connecting a display device that supports 1680x1050 to a system running Linux may result in a maximum display resolution of 1280x1024 only being available". (from thread) 

[It works with the latest driver] 

Fglrx driver does not detect some monitors correctly, so you have to do it manually (google about your monitor spec with keyword: xorg.conf). 

For example: Samsung 205BW 1680x1050 monitor needs manually set: File: /etc/X11/xorg.conf 
Section "Monitor"
       Option "DPMS"
       Identifier "samsung-monitor"
       VendorName "Samsung"
       ModelName "SyncMaster 205BW"
       HorizSync 31.4 - 80.0
       VertRefresh 56.000 - 75.000
       Modeline "1680x1050" 146.25 1680 1784 1960 2240 1050 1053 1059 1089
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"samsung-screen"
	Device		"samsung-device"
	Monitor		"samsung-monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

You may have to modify it a bit for your monitor and **remember to backup your xorg.conf.**

;)

---
